danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary defensive installations visible on terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zones; player must avoid or destroy to proceed safely

danger_signals.visual_form: Mobile ground-based military units displayed in overhead view
danger_signals.meaning: Mobile enemy presence; dynamic threat that tracks and engages player

danger_signals.visual_form: Large weapons platforms positioned across jungle and urban environments
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage threat requiring tactical avoidance or destruction priority

danger_signals.visual_form: Grouped hostile units in overhead perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Coordinated defensive positions; areas of concentrated danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel meter depleting over mission time
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity threat; failure to refuel results in craft crash and life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Armor bar depleting as helicopter sustains hits
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative danger state; zero armor triggers vehicle destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated extraction/delivery areas marked on terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Safe locations for completing objectives; helicopter fully protected at landing
Immediate damage mitigation; restores hull integrity

safety_signals.visual_form: Harvestable fuel resources positioned throughout environment
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment; extends mission capability and prevents crash
Objective completion also restores armor; dual safety/progress function
Sustains offensive capability; prevents ammunition starvation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Comanche attack helicopter as primary vehicle
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Washington D.C. locations; domestic US infrastructure as protected objectives

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nuclear weapons program; connection to Desert Strike antagonist (revenge motive)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy soldiers and anti-aircraft units deployed in desert and jungle settings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle fortress hideout; deserted island nuclear test site; international terror infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: South American drug trafficking network; nuclear bomb trucks
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cartel soldiers, armored vehicles, artillery positioned in jungle and South American territories
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle fortress; drug lord compound; South American landscape progression; nuclear bomb delivery vehicles

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Presidential limousine; White House as final objective location
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Washington D.C.; government infrastructure requiring protection; civilian population in danger

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active terrorist threat to US homeland
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opening level set in Washington D.C. with enemy forces attacking presidential convoy and urban centers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player helicopter must defend against immediate threats; enemies actively assault government targets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression through jungle environments with fortress installations; international scope shown through location changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transitions from defensive (protect DC) to offensive (destroy enemy bases) gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nuclear bomb trucks visible in final levels; explosion sequences in opening sequence; weapons platforms throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final level objective explicitly requires stopping nuclear bomb trucks before reaching White House

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opening cinematic shows Kilbaba and Ortega discussing attack; penultimate level features dual hideouts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kilbaba and Ortega pursue escape in final level; personal vendetta escalates to global threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: South American jungle setting; cartel military installations; oil tanker threat (PC version Alaska level)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player systematically dismantles cartel infrastructure; captures/eliminates Ortega

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mission progression from defense to offense
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level 1 defense in Washington D.C.; levels 2-4 counter-attacks on enemy bases; final return to D.C. for climactic defense
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player starts reactive (defending targets); transitions to proactive (destroying enemy installations); concludes with final defensive stand

environmental_storytelling.example: Opening cinematic set on deserted island showing nuclear test explosion establishes existential threat; subsequent jungle levels show enemy manufacturing/staging infrastructure
Early missions involve rescuing hostages and defending landmarks (storytelling through protection); later missions focus on base destruction (storytelling through offensive engagement)
Primary vehicle is US military attack helicopter; commandeering F-117 stealth jet reinforces advanced US military capability narrative; motorbike/hovercraft represent tactical flexibility
Presence of anti-aircraft guns, artillery, armored vehicles, and fortress installations visually communicates sophisticated military threat; escalation of threat complexity shows growing danger

Movement from Washington D.C. (homeland defense) → jungle territories (international counter-operations) → final return to D.C. (ultimate threat containment) creates three-act structure through environment alone
Nuclear explosion in opening; nuclear bomb trucks in final level; nuclear-themed fortress; weapons become increasingly central visual element as narrative climaxes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full maneuverability; can engage all enemy types; optimal offensive positioning available
Sustained damage vulnerability increases; strategic avoidance becomes necessary; behavior shifts from aggressive to defensive
Mission duration becomes critical factor; player must locate fuel barrels or abort; behavior becomes resource-management focused
Tracks player helicopter; fires when in range; stationary defensive posture
Mobile pursuit of player; active firing engagement; attempts to close distance
Static; awaits extraction; airlifting triggers armor restoration mechanic
Flee toward hideouts (penultimate level); escape attempts after prison break (final level); active threats with special status

notes: Jungle Strike communicates world-state primarily through location progression (domestic→international→domestic) and objective type shifts (defense→offense→defense). Faction identity relies heavily on narrative exposition (opening cinematic) rather than visual distinction—player identifies antagonists through story context rather than visual markers. The overhead perspective with 2.5D rendering creates visual clarity for threat assessment. Danger signals are primarily functional (enemy type/position) rather than atmospheric. Political situation (terrorism, drug trafficking, nuclear proliferation, revenge plot) communicated through objectives and cinematics rather than environmental visual language. Game emphasizes strategic gameplay communication (resource management via fuel/ammo/armor bars) over faction visual differentiation.
